title: How attention works in transformers
content:
Transformers are a class of neural network architectures that have become foundational in modern deep learning. The core mechanism, attention, allows each position in a sequence to weigh information from every other position when computing its output. This is in contrast to recurrent networks, which process sequences token by token.

Self-attention computes three projections of each input vector: queries, keys, and values. The dot product of queries and keys yields a similarity score, which is normalised via softmax and used to weight the values.

Multi-head attention extends this by running several attention computations in parallel, each with its own learned projections.
